content: pi-kit skills live under /Users/cwilson/.pi/agent/git/github.com/cwilson613/pi-kit/skills/
pi-kit themes/verdant.json defines the primary color palette used for Excalidraw semantic colors and TUI; see themes/verdant.json

The `style` skill (`skills/style/SKILL.md`) is the canonical Verdant design system — unified palette, typography, spacing, and rendering defaults shared across pi TUI theme (`themes/verdant.json`), Excalidraw semantic colors (`extensions/diffuse/excalidraw/types.ts`), Mermaid diagrams, and FLUX.1 image generation.
extensions/style.ts registers /style command; embeds Verdant palette tokens directly (no file read at runtime). Registered in package.json pi.extensions array.

pi skill commands autocomplete as /skill:name (requires enableSkillCommands:true in settings.json). Extension commands registered via pi.registerCommand() are a separate namespace and may not autocomplete the same way.
extensions/style.ts /style d2 subcommand replaced /style mermaid — renders D2 Verdant style template block. MERMAID_INIT constant replaced with D2_STYLE_TEMPLATE.
skills/style/SKILL.md and skills/visualize/SKILL.md updated: Mermaid references removed, D2 is now the canonical structured diagram tool with full syntax reference and Verdant styling examples.

pi-kit visualize skill covers D2 and Excalidraw diagram rendering; FLUX.1 for image generation. Excalidraw renderer at skills/visualize/references/excalidraw/. Extension at extensions/render/index.ts (renamed from diffuse). Mermaid has been removed.

render_diagram tool in extensions/render/index.ts uses D2: pi.exec('d2') with elk layout, theme 200, --pad 40, saves .d2 source + PNG to ~/.pi/visuals/. Supports layout/theme/sketch params. Mermaid/mmdc code removed.

render_diagram tool uses D2 as diagram renderer. D2 v0.7.1 installed via Nix. Tool lives in extensions/render/index.ts (renamed from diffuse), uses elk layout + theme 200 by default, outputs PNG to ~/.pi/visuals/.

Excalidraw element factories vendored into extensions/render/excalidraw/elements.ts; semantic palette in extensions/render/excalidraw/types.ts. 11 SemanticPurpose roles. roughness defaults to 0 (clean), fontFamily 3 (Cascadia).

Excalidraw semantic palette: 11 roles (primary/secondary/tertiary/start/end/decision/ai/warning/error/evidence/inactive) mapped to fill/stroke hex pairs. Defined in extensions/render/excalidraw/types.ts.

pi-kit features only exist if they are committed and pushed to the git remote (github.com/cwilson613/pi-kit). Work that isn't committed and pushed upstream does not exist — never assume a skill, extension, or config is present based on session memory alone. Always verify against the actual repo state.
Mermaid/mmdc replaced by D2 for all diagram rendering in pi-kit. D2 is the canonical diagramming language going forward — never use Mermaid syntax in render_diagram calls.

D2 (d2lang.com) replaced Mermaid as the primary diagram renderer. The `render_diagram` tool in `extensions/diffuse/index.ts` now invokes the `d2` CLI (--theme 200, --layout elk) instead of mmdc. D2 is installed via Nix at v0.7.1, renders PNG natively in ~300ms. All Mermaid references removed from skills and extensions.
extensions/diffuse renamed to extensions/render across all files and package.json; merged via PR refactor/rename-diffuse-to-render and pushed to main.

style skill had no extension command handler; fixed by adding extensions/style.ts with pi.registerCommand() — /style now dispatches: (no args) quick ref card, palette Mermaid swatch, mermaid init block, excalidraw table, check <file> color audit, free text delegates to agent.

/style palette does fuzzy-match fallthrough: typo 'pallete' hits default case and delegates as freeform question instead of routing to palette subcommand. Needs fuzzy/alias matching in extensions/style.ts handler.
enableSkillCommands is not set in ~/.pi/agent/settings.json — /skill:style and other skill commands will not autocomplete until it is enabled.
extensions/view/index.ts still references Mermaid (.mmd/.mermaid file handling, mmdc optional dep) — not yet cleaned up as of the rename PR merge.
extensions/style.ts still lists 'mermaid' as a subcommand in its header comment — stale after D2 migration.

title: Added missing style skill and upstream constraint
narrative: The session goal was to create and publish a unified visual style guide skill (`skills/style/SKILL.md`) for the pi-kit project that had been planned but never committed. The full Verdant design system was documented — covering the core color palette, Excalidraw semantic fill/stroke pairs, Mermaid dark theme init block, typography scale, spacing grid, and rendering defaults for Excalidraw, Mermaid, and FLUX.1. The skill was committed and pushed to the remote, making it live. A core constraint was also added to memory: features that haven't been committed and pushed upstream do not exist, preventing future sessions from assuming work is present based on session context alone.

title: Built and Fixed the Verdant Style Skill
narrative: The session goal was to complete and ship the `/style` skill — a unified Verdant design system reference for pi-kit. The style skill had been built in a prior session but never committed, so it was first written and pushed to the remote. A constraint was also codified in memory: features not committed and pushed upstream don't exist. After initial deployment, the user discovered that invoking `/style` did nothing visible — the skill was a pure reference document with no agent behavior instructions. A dispatch table was added to SKILL.md defining responses for direct invocation (no args, palette, mermaid, excalidraw, check), committed, and pushed. The skill is now live and functional.

title: Converted /style skill into working extension command
narrative: The goal was to make `/style` actually do something when invoked, rather than silently loading a SKILL.md into agent context with no visible output. After back-and-forth where the agent kept adding dispatch table documentation to the skill file (which still produced no user-visible output), the user correctly pointed out that skills are passive context injectors and a command handler was needed. The agent created `extensions/style.ts` registering a `pi.registerCommand('style', ...)` handler with subcommand dispatch: direct display for quick-ref/mermaid/excalidraw/check, and `sendUserMessage` delegation for palette rendering and freeform questions. The extension was registered in `package.json` and pushed. The SKILL.md dispatch table instructions added in the prior commit are now redundant dead text, though that cleanup was not addressed.

title: Replaced Mermaid/mmdc with D2 diagram support in pi-kit
narrative: The goal was to fully purge Mermaid and mmdc references from pi-kit's view and render extensions in favor of D2. The assistant updated file extension handlers (.mmd/.mermaid → .d2), rendering logic (mmdc CLI → d2 CLI with elk layout), and all associated doc comments across view/index.ts and render/index.ts. A grep confirmed no stray Mermaid/mmdc references remained except the intentional 'NOT Mermaid' prompt guideline. Changes were committed and pushed to main in a single clean commit.
